question: what is hhv? explain in details
answer: HHV stands for Higher Heating Value, which is the heat release including water droplet condensation

generated.question: what is hhv? explain in details
generated.answer: HHV stands for Higher Heating Value, which is the heat release including water droplet condensation

chunk.text:
Extraction Turbine (CET)
■ Impulse & Reaction Turbines
58. In Impulse Turbine, the enthalpy of steam is fully converted to __________ steam jet through
nozzles before blades.
Ans: high velocity
59. For Simple Impulse Turbine, the exit velocity is very high — about __________ of inlet velocity.
Ans: 33%
60. In Pressure Compounding (Rateau Turbine), total pressure drop of steam occurs in successive
rows of __________.
Ans: nozzles
61. In Velocity Compounding (Curtis Turbine), the whole pressure drop occurs in a __________
row of nozzle.
Ans: single
62. The function of Fixed Blades in a Curtis Turbine is only to change the __________ of the steam
coming out from MB1.
Ans: direction
63. In Reaction Turbine, power is generated due to __________ force out of pressure drop inside
the moving part.

possible __________ mixing with air.
Ans: better
19. Excess air contributes to __________ gas loss as it carries heat away from the fuel to the
chimney.
Ans: Exhaust
20. The intersection point of decreasing incomplete combustion loss and increasing exhaust gas
loss is called the __________ of the A/F ratio.
Ans: Operating Zone
■ Calorific Value (HHV & LHV)
21. The heat release including water droplet condensation is called the __________.
Ans: HHV — Higher Heating Value
22. If the final exit of flue gas is without condensation of water vapour, the corresponding lower
value is known as __________.
Ans: LHV — Lower Heating Value
23. LHV = HHV − __________.
Ans: mw x hfg
24. The dew point temperature is also called __________.
Ans: Acid-Condensation Temperature
25. In power plants, the exit

53. In turbines, steam does not get __________ with the lubricating oil as no lubricating oil is used
in the steam space.
Ans: contaminated
■ Classification of Steam Turbines
54. On the basis of steam input, turbines may be __________ pressure or __________ pressure.
Ans: Single, Dual/Multi
55. If the whole amount of steam from turbine goes to condenser, it is called __________ Turbine.
Ans: Condensing
56. If whole steam passes through a process heater for utility heating, it is called __________
Turbine.
Ans: Back Pressure
57. If a small part of steam is extracted for process heating and the rest passes through
condenser, it is called __________ Turbine.
Ans: Condensing Extraction Turbine (CET)
■ Impulse & Reaction Turbines
58. In Impulse Turbine, the enthalpy of steam is fully converted
